title: Stott (Appellant) v Thomas Cook Tour Operators Limited (Respondent)

metadata.title: Stott (Appellant) v Thomas Cook Tour Operators Limited (Respondent)
metadata.summary: 1. This appeal arises from a sorry case of a serious failure by an air tour operator to see that proper provision was made for the needs of a disabled passenger, contrary to the requirements of the Civil Aviation (Access to Air Travel for Disabled Persons and Persons with Reduced Mobility) Regula...
extracts: 1. This appeal arises from a sorry case of a serious failure by an air tour operator to see that proper provision was made for the needs of a disabled passenger, contrary to the requirements of the Civil Aviation (Access to Air Travel for Disabled Persons and Persons with Reduced Mobility) Regulations 2007 (SI 2007/1895) (“the UK Disability Regulations”).

2. The UK Disability Regulations implement Regulation (EC) No 1107/2006 of the European Parliament and the Council concerning the rights of disabled persons and persons with reduced mobility when travelling by air (“the EC Disability Regulation”).
metadata.title_key: stott v thomas cook tour operators limited
